pred_reason: SWC-101 is applicable due to identified reentrancy vulnerabilities in external calls like _safeMint without proper state updates, while SWC-107 is justified by the use of transfer() in withdraw which limits gas and could cause failures. Evidence: Reentrancy risk in _safeMint calls without Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern; transfer() in withdraw forwards only 2300 gas, risking failures with gas-consuming fallbacks; High severity findings confirm external call vulnerabilities and gas limitation issues

raw.pred_reason: SWC-101 is applicable due to identified reentrancy vulnerabilities in external calls like _safeMint without proper state updates, while SWC-107 is justified by the use of transfer() in withdraw which limits gas and could cause failures. Evidence: Reentrancy risk in _safeMint calls without Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern; transfer() in withdraw forwards only 2300 gas, risking failures with gas-consuming fallbacks; High severity findings confirm external call vulnerabilities and gas limitation issues

llm_explanation.reason: SWC-101 is applicable due to identified reentrancy vulnerabilities in external calls like _safeMint without proper state updates, while SWC-107 is justified by the use of transfer() in withdraw which limits gas and could cause failures.
evidence: Reentrancy risk in _safeMint calls without Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern
transfer() in withdraw forwards only 2300 gas, risking failures with gas-consuming fallbacks
High severity findings confirm external call vulnerabilities and gas limitation issues
